food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken burger with cheese, large fries, and soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken sandwich food product class; no specific McChicken with Cheese Upsized Meal entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken burger) is the closest defensible match for the burger component. The upsized meal includes fries and a drink; no composite meal code exists in FoodEx2 at this specificity.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps precisely to a branded fast food combo meal. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (McChicken with Cheese burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a McChicken with Cheese burger (approximately 175 g), a large portion of french fries (approximately 175 g), and a large soft drink (approximately 400 ml / 400 g), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McChicken with Cheese, Large Fries, Large Soft Drink (Upsized Meal components)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) used for component-level estimation; McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data used as primary reference for burger and fries components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for the McChicken with Cheese burger and for large fries separately. The upsized meal is a composite of: McChicken with Cheese burger (~175 g, ~430 kcal), large fries (~175 g, ~490 kcal), and a large Coca-Cola (~400 ml, ~160 kcal). Per-100g values are derived by dividing total meal nutrients by total meal weight (750 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for 'Fast foods, chicken sandwich' (NDB 21120) and 'Fast foods, potato, french fried' (NDB 21137) were used to cross-validate macro ratios. Soft drink component estimated from USDA FoodData Central branded Coca-Cola data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for fast food chicken burger meal with refined-carbohydrate components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McChicken with Cheese Upsized Meal as a composite. GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted average of the meal's glycemic-carbohydrate contributors: white sesame bun and breaded coating (~GI 70–75, analogous to white bread / refined wheat products per University of Sydney GI Database), large french fries (~GI 63, University of Sydney GI Database entry for McDonald's french fries, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283), and cola soft drink (~GI 63, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 144 g) / 100 = 93.6. Reported GL per serving is 93.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1,860 mg sodium per full upsized meal serving (248 mg per 100 g × 750 g), representing over 80% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal due to its very high sodium load from the seasoned chicken patty, processed cheese, fries, and condiments; if consumed, it should replace all other high-sodium foods for that day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 94) driven by refined-carbohydrate components including the white sesame bun, breaded coating, large french fries, and sugar-sweetened soft drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal due to its very high glycemic load and rapidly digestible carbohydrates from the bun, breading, fries, and cola, which are likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the soft drink with water and the large fries with a side salad would substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high saturated fat (from cheese, chicken skin, and frying oil), high sodium, high trans fat (trace), and high total energy density in a single meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, as its saturated fat content (~13.5 g per meal), high sodium (~1,860 mg), and ultra-processed nature are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol, arterial stiffness, and increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McChicken with Cheese Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food combo meal consisting of a McChicken with Cheese burger, a large portion of french fries, and a large soft drink. The McChicken burger features a crispy deep-fried breaded chicken patty topped with shredded lettuce, creamy mayonnaise, and a slice of processed American-style cheese, all served in a soft sesame seed bun. The upsized (large) format increases the fries and drink portions compared to the standard meal. The burger patty is seasoned and coated in a light, crunchy breadcrumb batter before deep-frying, giving it a golden exterior and juicy interior. The meal is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 1,090 kcal per serving, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (bun, fries, cola), saturated fat (cheese, frying oil), and sodium (seasoning, cheese, condiments). The glycemic load per serving is very high (approximately 94), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~1,860 mg per meal) is a major concern for hypertension management. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed only occasionally as part of a balanced diet. It is a popular fast food option in Singapore, widely available at McDonald's outlets island-wide, and is commonly ordered via field survey and other delivery platforms.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a McChicken with Cheese Upsized Meal?
Is the McChicken Upsized Meal halal in Singapore?
What is the sodium content of McDonald's McChicken meal?
Can diabetics eat a McChicken Upsized Meal?
What is the glycemic index of McDonald's McChicken burger?
How much fat is in a McChicken with Cheese large meal?
McDonald's upsized meal nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for the McChicken with Cheese burger and large fries on their official nutrition page, which serves as the primary reference. However, the upsized meal as a composite unit is not published as a single nutrition entry; per-100g values are derived by summing component nutrients and dividing by total meal weight. The soft drink component is estimated from USDA FoodData Central branded cola data. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for component foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are derived from the composite meal (McChicken with Cheese burger ~175 g + large fries ~175 g + large cola ~400 g = ~750 g total). Component weights are approximate based on McDonald's Singapore published serving sizes. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition data or the USDA SR Legacy fast food entries used for cross-validation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data for this composite meal. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data. 5. GI of 65 is a weighted analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific composite meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. GL per serving is calculated as (65 × 144 g available carbohydrate per 750 g serving) / 100 = 93.6. 7. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed', as this is a composite fast food meal containing chicken, bread, potato, and dairy components. 8. Halal status confirmed per McDonald's Singapore halal certification (MUIS-certified). 9. Trans fat is reported as 0.1 g per 100 g, reflecting trace trans fats from frying oils; this is not zero and is supported by USDA SR Legacy data for similar fast food items.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from input value 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed' to accurately reflect the composite meal nature.
Derived per-100g nutrient values from composite meal component weights using McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data.
Computed GI as weighted analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component entries.
Computed GL per serving as 93.6 based on 144 g available carbohydrate in 750 g serving at GI 65.
Added all mandatory multilingual local names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) for Singaporean cuisine category.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed enum values.
Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed enum values.
Added the romanized alias 'Mai Ji Dan Bao Jia Zhi Si Chao Zhi Tao Can' to the flat 'aliases' list to ensure consistency with 'aliases_by_language.romanized', resolving the Q8 failure.
Set ontology codes to closest_parent matches with honest gap notes; no codes invented.
Populated full health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
